input.headline: Why nonalcoholic spirits go from strength to strength

raw.headline: Why nonalcoholic spirits go from strength to strength

winner: The bartender said, "I can't serve you any alcohol tonight, sir." I asked, "Why's that?" He replied, "Well, I've got this new nonalcoholic whiskey that'll put hair on your chest and make you forget your own name. But it's still in the bottle.

candidates.text: I told my friend I was giving up alcohol, so he said, "Cool! You can finally enjoy a *spirit* without any *liquor* in your life.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "The joke is concise, uses wordplay on 'spirit' and 'liquor,' and ties directly to the headline with a clever twist."}

candidates.text: Why did the nonalcoholic spirit refuse to play cards with anyone? Because they always went from strength to strength—and left the deck behind!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Puns on 'strength to strength' and 'left the deck behind' are clever and specific, with a natural flow and surprising twist."}

candidates.text: They say non-alcoholic spirits are rising like a phoenix from the ashes, but honestly, I'm just glad they're not rising *in* me like whiskey used to.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising twist on the phoenix metaphor, specific to nonalcoholic spirits, and naturally funny with a relatable comparison to whiskey's effects."}

candidates.text: I told my friend about the rise of non-alcoholic spirits, he said "Yeah, that's great—just what the world needed, more reasons for people to feel like they're being punished without the punishment.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "The joke is concise, surprising, and specific, with a clever twist on the idea of 'punishment without punishment.' It uses natural English and effectively ties into the headline's theme."}

candidates.text: The bartender said, "I can't serve you any alcohol tonight, sir." I asked, "Why's that?" He replied, "Well, I've got this new nonalcoholic whiskey that'll put hair on your chest and make you forget your own name. But it's still in the bottle.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke has some humor with the unexpected twist about the nonalcoholic whiskey, but it's a bit long and the punchline could be more concise and surprising."}
